title: podman denies opening a file for writing as root owned by a different user on /tmp
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman denies opening a file for writing as root owned by a different user on /tmp

(2500661 4 drwxrwxrwt 1 root root 4096 Dec  5 16:56 /tmp)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. start container as root: ``podman run --rm -it centos:centos8 /bin/bash``

2. create a file on /tmp, change ownership, open file for writing

``touch /tmp/a && chown mail:mail /tmp/a && vi /tmp/a`` _(actually write something to it in vi and exit :x)_ - **fails**

or

``touch /tmp/a && chown mail:mail /tmp/a && python3 -c "open('/tmp/a', 'w')"`` - **fails**
(but requires installing python3 beforehand: ``dnf install -y python3``

whereas shell redirection works

``touch /tmp/a && chown mail:mail /tmp/a && echo "foo" > /tmp/a`` - **succeeds**

3. do the same steps on docker - **succeeds**

**Describe the results you received:**

``PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/tmp/a'``

**Describe the results you expected:**

_changed file contents_

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

* selinux on fedora-30 Podman host is disabled

```
[root@fedora-30 ~]# getenforce
Disabled
```

* disabling seccomp doesn't resolve this issue

```
podman run --security-opt aaseccomp=unconfined --rm -it centos:centos8 /bin/bash
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.10
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

fedora-30 VM is running on macOS Parallels host system

comments.body: @rhatdan @baude @vrothberg @giuseppe PTAL - looked at this one for a while in IRC, came away stumped

> 3. do the same steps on docker - **succeeds**

I just installed docker on my f30 VM and tried again and it **fails** as well whereas it doesn't fail in my docker environment on my macOS host. Must be an issue with the configuration of the host system though.
Just to double check: it only fails in vi when trying to write? Opening works?

Yes and shell redirection does:

```
openat(AT_FDCWD, "a", O_WRONLY|O_CREAT|O_TRUNC, 0666) = -1 EACCES (Permission denied)
openat(AT_FDCWD, "a", O_WRONLY|O_TRUNC) = 3
```

apparently this fails on the fedora-30 host in general

``touch /tmp/a && chown mail:mail /tmp/a && vi /tmp/a``

or

``touch /tmp/a && chown mail:mail /tmp/a && python3 -c "open('/tmp/a', 'w')"``

that is controlled by the `fs.protected_regular` sysctl.

Can you try `sysctl fs.protected_regular=0` and see if it changes the result you get?
@giuseppe This resolves the issue on the host as well as on the container. Thanks.
This is not a podman issue.
